evals.prompt: Review this file for me: routes/deals.py
evals.expected_output: Structured review with findings grouped by severity (Critical, Warning, Suggestion), each with location, issue, why-it-matters explanation, and suggested fix
expectations: Reads the target file before reviewing
Applies 5 review dimensions: Security, Correctness, Readability, Patterns, Beginner Pitfalls
Each finding includes a why-it-matters explanation
Starts with positive observations before listing issues
Uses the severity format: CRITICAL, WARNING, SUGGESTION

evals.prompt: What's wrong with this code? Check my work please
evals.expected_output: Beginner-friendly review with educational tone, explaining issues as learning opportunities
expectations: Asks which file or code to review if not specified
Explains why each issue matters with concrete examples
Keeps findings proportional to code size

evals.prompt: Review PR #42 and explain issues for a junior developer
evals.expected_output: PR diff review with beginner-friendly explanations for each finding
expectations: Fetches PR diff via gh pr diff
Includes learn-more explanations for each finding

evals.expected_output: Asks user to verify the file path
expectations: Does not hallucinate a code review
Asks user to check the file path or provide the correct one
